uniqueFacts: The Creek Council House in downtown Okmulgee, built in 1878 from native sandstone, served as the Muscogee (Creek) Nation capitol from 1868 until Oklahoma statehood in 1907, and was designated a National Historic Landmark in 1961.
During the 1920s oil boom, Okmulgee had five operational oil refineries and was reported to have more millionaires per capita than any other city of comparable size in the United States.

Dripping Springs Lake, constructed in 1975 by the City of Okmulgee, was designated as Oklahoma's first official 'Trophy Bass Lake' and is renowned among anglers for its exceptional largemouth bass fishing.
The Deep Fork National Wildlife Refuge in southern Okmulgee County was established in 1993 and now encompasses over 9,000 acres of forested wetlands, providing habitat for 254 observed bird species.

Okmulgee County was formed on July 16, 1907, at Oklahoma statehood from land that had belonged to the Muscogee (Creek) Nation, with county government offices originally located within the Creek Council House until the present courthouse was built in 1916.
processServingNotes: Okmulgee County is located entirely within the Muscogee (Creek) Nation Reservation. Following the McGirt v. Oklahoma decision, tribal jurisdiction may apply to certain criminal matters involving Native Americans. Process servers should coordinate with both state and tribal courts when necessary.

The Okmulgee County Courthouse at 314 W 7th St serves the 24th Judicial District. Filings and process serving should confirm venue requirements, particularly for matters involving tribal members or land within the Muscogee Nation boundaries.
Over 4,000 workers commute into Okmulgee County daily from surrounding counties, making workplace service of process an important consideration for serving employees who may live outside the county.
heroLead: Nestled in east-central Oklahoma within the Muscogee (Creek) Nation Reservation, Okmulgee County blends rich Native American heritage with a thriving manufacturing base and world-class fishing at Dripping Springs Lake. From the historic Creek Council House in downtown Okmulgee to the nine global-exporting manufacturers driving the local economy, our process servers cover all 702 square miles of this diverse county. Call (539) 367-6832 or visit /contact to schedule service.

faqs.question: What tribal jurisdiction covers Okmulgee County?
faqs.answer: Okmulgee County is located entirely within the Muscogee (Creek) Nation Reservation. The Muscogee Nation's capital and seat of government is in Okmulgee, and the historic Creek Council House serves as both a museum and a symbol of tribal sovereignty. The McGirt v. Oklahoma decision affirmed that the Muscogee Nation's reservation was never disestablished. For process serving assistance in Okmulgee County, including tribal territories, call Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.

faqs.question: What is the population and geography of Okmulgee County?
faqs.answer: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, Okmulgee County had an estimated population of 37,084 as of July 2024. The county covers 702 square miles in east-central Oklahoma and includes the county seat of Okmulgee along with incorporated communities such as Henryetta, Beggs, Morris, Dewar, Grayson, Hoffman, Liberty, Schulter, and Winchester. The county is approximately 60.9% White, 16.9% American Indian, and 7.6% Black.

faqs.question: What does the name 'Okmulgee' mean and where did it come from?
faqs.answer: The name Okmulgee is derived from the Hitchita (Lower Creek) word 'okimulgi,' meaning 'boiling waters.' It was taken from a Creek town of the same name in the tribe's native homeland in present-day Russell County, Alabama. After the Creek Nation was forcibly removed to Indian Territory along the Trail of Tears, they established their new capital at Okmulgee in 1868. A U.S. Post Office opened there on April 29, 1869.

faqs.question: What are the major industries and employers in Okmulgee County?
faqs.answer: Manufacturing is a cornerstone of Okmulgee County's economy, with nine value-added manufacturers including five that ship products globally. Major employers include the Muscogee Creek Nation government, Oklahoma State University Institute of Technology (OSUIT) with 600+ employees, the Muscogee Creek Nation Medical Center (400+ employees), Hillcrest Hospital Henryetta, CP Kelco manufacturing, and Walmart. Healthcare and education are also significant employment sectors.

faqs.question: When was the Okmulgee County Courthouse built and what are its details?
faqs.answer: The current Okmulgee County Courthouse at 314 W 7th St was constructed in 1916. Prior to that, county government offices were located in the Creek Council House from 1907 to 1916. The courthouse serves as the seat of the 24th Judicial District and houses the District Court Clerk's office, which can be reached at (918) 756-3042. Hours are Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM. For process serving services in Okmulgee County, contact Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.

faqs.question: What are some unique attractions and events in Okmulgee County?
faqs.answer: Dripping Springs Lake was Oklahoma's first designated Trophy Bass Lake and offers world-class fishing. The Creek Council House Museum, a National Historic Landmark, showcases Muscogee Nation history. The Deep Fork National Wildlife Refuge protects over 9,000 acres of wetlands habitat. The annual Okmulgee Pecan Festival celebrates the county's agricultural heritage with food, crafts, and entertainment. One Fire Casino offers gaming, and the Okmulgee Downtown Historic District features preserved early 20th-century architecture. Call (539) 367-6832 for local process serving needs.
